sentence_reviews.text: Sampling, filtering, spectra, Fourier analysis, and wavelets characterize structured signals across time and space.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Digital signal processing page: processed signals are sample sequences representing continuous variables in time, space, or frequency (lines 172-175); signal sampling, filtering, time/space domains, Fourier/frequency-domain analysis, spectral analysis, and wavelet-domain discussion (lines 182-228). Wavelet page: wavelets are useful for signal processing, audio, and images, and wavelet theory is related to harmonic analysis (search result inspected).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected evidence supports the listed signal-processing components and time/space/frequency framing, but the exact summary verb 'characterize' is synthesized across sections and sources rather than directly stated.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Signal processing & harmonic analysis is to connect Linear algebra, Calculus & automatic differentiation theory to downstream capabilities including Neuroscience & cognitive science, Sequences, events & time series, Images, video & spatial grids.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA site: mathematical background, uncertainty/probabilistic reasoning, machine learning, computer vision, and robotics appear in the table of contents (lines 36-58). Digital signal processing page: DSP uses sample sequences in time, space, or frequency (lines 172-175), includes audio/speech, image/video, biomedical, and other applications (lines 247-264), and treats time, space, frequency, and wavelet domains (lines 191-228).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support signal processing's relevance to time/space signals, images/video, audio/speech, biomedical applications, and mathematical background. They do not directly support the exact atlas bridge claim, the full downstream list, or the automatic-differentiation part of the calculus dependency.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Digital signal processing page: lists biomedical engineering, medical imaging, hearing aid technology, speech/audio processing, and related applications (lines 247-264).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected evidence supports biomedical and sensory-signal applications, but it does not directly establish neuroscience and cognitive science as downstream dependencies of signal processing.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Sequences, events & time series
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Digital signal processing page: digital signals are sequences of samples in time, space, or frequency (lines 172-175), and time-domain analysis and sample sequences are discussed (lines 191-199). Discrete Fourier transform page: spectral analysis uses uniformly spaced time samples of a signal and can create spectrograms (search result and opened page inspected).
sentence_reviews.notes: The evidence supports signal processing over temporal sample sequences and spectral/time-frequency analysis. The word 'substrate' is an atlas synthesis rather than a direct source phrase.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Images, video & spatial grids
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Digital signal processing page: signals can be represented in spatial domains (lines 172-175), space-domain analysis includes pixel location for image processing (lines 191-198), and image/video applications are listed (lines 247-264).
sentence_reviews.notes: The evidence supports spatial-domain and image/video signal processing. The exact 'substrate for' relationship is graph-derived wording.

sentence_reviews.text: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting. Its direct dependencies include Linear algebra, Calculus & automatic differentiation theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Digital signal processing page: engineers choose the processing domain by making an informed assumption about which domain best represents the signal and processing (lines 191-192); practical sampling and filtering limitations are described (lines 182-187).
sentence_reviews.notes: The evidence supports that DSP relies on modeling/domain assumptions and has practical sampling/filtering limitations. It does not directly support the empirical-mismatch warning, and it does not support the exact dependency list or the automatic-differentiation phrase.

sentence_reviews.text: Signal processing & harmonic analysis rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Linear algebra.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Digital signal processing page: digital filtering generally consists of linear transformations and convolution (lines 197-199), and Fourier/frequency-domain processing is central (lines 204-208). Discrete Fourier transform page: the DFT is a linear transform and can be written using a DFT matrix (opened page inspected, Definition/Properties sections).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support linear transformations, convolution, Fourier transforms, and matrix formulations as central computational machinery. The exact 'rests on' phrasing is a synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: Signal processing & harmonic analysis rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Calculus & automatic differentiation theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Digital signal processing page: sampling converts continuous signals to discrete models (lines 182-187), Fourier transforms convert time/space information to frequency-domain components (lines 204-208), and time-frequency uncertainty is discussed (lines 222-228). Wavelet transform page: wavelet transforms are expressed with integrals over continuous signals and time/frequency parameters (search result inspected).
sentence_reviews.notes: The evidence supports calculus-adjacent continuous transforms, sampling, and time-frequency analysis. It does not support automatic differentiation theory as part of this dependency, so the exact rationale is broader than the inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Signal processing & harmonic analysis rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Probability theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Digital signal processing page: lists statistical signal processing and spectral density estimation among DSP applications (lines 175 and 247-264). Wiener filter page: describes estimating a target random process from an observed noisy process using known stationary signal and noise spectra (opened page inspected).
sentence_reviews.notes: The evidence supports probability/statistics as important in statistical signal processing and noisy-signal filtering, but it does not show probability theory as a universal requirement for signal processing and harmonic analysis. The edge strength is common rather than core, which fits the partial support better than this sentence's 'rests on' wording.

edge_reviews.notes: Filtering as linear transformation/convolution and DFT matrix/linear-transform formulations support linear algebra as a core prerequisite, though no inspected source states the atlas edge in exactly this wording.

Continuous signals, Fourier transforms, sampling, and wavelet integrals support calculus as important to signal processing and harmonic analysis. The dependency label's automatic-differentiation component is not supported by the inspected evidence, so the exact edge is broader than the sources justify.

Statistical signal processing, spectral density estimation, and Wiener filtering support probability as a common dependency for noisy or statistical signal-processing work. The sources do not support probability as core for all signal processing, so the packet's common strength is defensible.
